continue: Weight is trending nicely toward goal — 170.8 lb today, down steadily from ~174-175 lb three weeks ago, a real step toward the low-160s target.

Recovery markers are all green — HRV balanced at 54ms, RHR a strong 45 this morning, training readiness 79 ("well recovered"), ACWR optimal at 0.9. The body is fully absorbing the current load and has room for more.
The one run that did happen this week (Jul 6, 4.5 mi) was genuinely easy at HR 121 — good execution of true Z2 pacing whenever running does happen.
change: Polarized split is 83% easy / 17% gray / 0% hard over the last 30 days — the gray zone kept shrinking, but there's been zero genuine hard/quality running in a month. This week's plan puts a real interval session back on the calendar (6x3min @ 6:20-6:40/mi, Wed).

Running volume has kept sliding for four straight weeks — 23.1 → 18.7 → 11.1 → 4.5 mi — with tennis filling the gap instead. Tennis is great cross-training, but it doesn't carry marathon-specific aerobic loading; next week's plan returns to ~26 mi to get back into the target maintenance band (18-32 mi/wk).

VO2max is flat around 52 for two months and the marathon prediction drifted slightly slower (3:43:03 → 3:44:07) — a direct consequence of the volume dip, not a fitness problem. Recovery is excellent, so consistent running should turn this back around quickly.
